title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deployable Go feed-service release unit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, secrets references, database compatibility, migration state, network policy, budgets, and dependency reachability before serving.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Progressive Rollout

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 6978 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.
